seoTitle: How to Report a Roth IRA Conversion in TurboTax
metaDescription: Learn how to enter a Roth IRA conversion in TurboTax so your income and tax implications are reported correctly this filing season.
intro: Converting a traditional IRA to a Roth IRA has real tax consequences, and reporting it incorrectly can throw off your whole return. This guide walks through how TurboTax handles Roth IRA conversions so you can report the income accurately and understand what it means for your taxes.

steps.heading: Understand why the conversion matters
steps.body: A Roth IRA conversion is generally a taxable event, since it involves moving pre-tax funds into an account that grows tax-free. Reporting it properly affects your taxable income for the year.

steps.heading: Gather your conversion documentation
steps.body: You'll need the forms your financial institution issued documenting the conversion, since TurboTax relies on this information to calculate the tax impact.

steps.heading: Enter the conversion in the retirement income section
steps.body: Locate the section of TurboTax dealing with IRA and retirement account activity, and enter the details of your conversion there.

steps.heading: Let TurboTax calculate the tax implications
steps.body: TurboTax uses automated calculations to determine how the conversion affects your taxable income, reducing the chance of manual math errors.

steps.heading: Review before filing
steps.body: Check the summary TurboTax generates for your conversion to confirm the income and tax figures match your actual conversion paperwork.

faq.question: Is a Roth IRA conversion always taxable?
faq.answer: In most cases, converting pre-tax traditional IRA funds to a Roth IRA creates taxable income for the year of the conversion, which TurboTax factors into your return.

faq.question: What documents do I need to report a Roth IRA conversion?
faq.answer: You'll need the tax forms your IRA custodian sends documenting the distribution and conversion, which TurboTax uses to calculate the reportable amount.

faq.question: Can TurboTax help beginners who've never reported a conversion before?
faq.answer: Yes, TurboTax's guided interface is built to walk both beginners and professionals through entering retirement account activity, including conversions.

faq.question: Will this affect my future retirement planning?
faq.answer: Yes, understanding how the conversion is taxed now can inform decisions about future contributions and conversions as part of your broader retirement strategy.
